topic: Verify ISS-2388 (defender-dialogic scope-limit sentence) + ISS-2390 (PRED-095 cherry-picking parenthetical) patches landed in §8b.3 L3a paragraph
change_summary: Decider commit 2df48ad applied two restorations to the §8b.3 L3a consolidated paragraph: (a) ISS-2388 — restored the defender-dialogic scope-limit sentence ("We accept the dome-defender scope-limit that monitor.py is internal tooling, not a public commitment; the critique narrows to the two issues that survive consolidation."); (b) ISS-2390 — restored a PRED-095 cherry-picking parenthetical attached to the "selection from coexisting dome formulas" clause ("(the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern: monitor.py selection)"). This c10 review is a narrow post-integration verification of those two patches.
our_argument_summary: §8b.3 Point 3 (eclipse audit) presents a four-layer structure (L1 ensemble, L2 timing, L3a magnetic falsifier, L3b gravity, L4 optics) and steel-mans the dome by adopting the layered audit format and codifying a Pass band. The post-EXP-462 consolidated L3a paragraph carries: codified Pass band, derivation chain ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, globe-fingerprint critique (WIN-025 + OPEN-001), outbound cross-references to §4.2.4/§4.2.5/§4.2.3/§6.14, defender-dialogic scope-limit narrowing, and the Fail-clause interpretation-A vs interpretation-B double-bind. The (d) Unchanged section restates that an ensemble anchor fit to its own data is OTS-wrong-side and that the magnitude Pass band does not arbitrate dome vs globe.

relevant_files: git show 2df48ad (decider patch commit applying both restorations)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: ISS-2388 + ISS-2390 are prose-restoration patches; no analytical claims or numerical values are changed. ISS-2388 reinstates the visible "defender objection → we accept scope-limit" move from PRE L3a #3. ISS-2390 reinstates a text reference to PRED-095's detail_reasoning cherry-picking concession.
data_sources_used: self-citing within sections.json; PRED-095 referenced by name in §8b.3 (no inline link present)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None introduced. The interpretation-A/B Fail-clause double-bind in the consolidated paragraph remains verbatim from PRE; the new defender-dialogic sentence does not weaken any falsification criterion — it accepts a narrowing of scope (monitor.py is internal tooling, not a public commitment) that was already implicit in c9's deeper analysis.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None for ISS-2388. For ISS-2390: the patch restores the text reference but NOT the inline PRED-095 detail reasoning link that the original EXP-462 c1 hole #4 recommendation explicitly called for. The phrase "monitor.py selection" in the restored parenthetical is also cryptic compared to the c1 long-form recommendation (which specified "monitor.py's −29.1 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the published W004 window").

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: §8b.3 is a methodology/audit section; not a per-WIN claim, so monitor.py does not test it. Inherits from L1 WIN-068, L2 WIN-011, L3a PRED-095, L3b PRED-TIER-2 individually.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is a steel-man audit framework, not a phenomenon claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section explicitly identifies which layers are post-hoc (L1) vs prospective (L3a PRED-095 registered 2026-03-12) vs recycled (L3b restating WIN-011).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section is review methodology, not a dome derivation.

holes_found.description: ISS-2390 patch is partial — the inline PRED-095 detail reasoning link recommended by the EXP-462 c1 H4 source hole is NOT present in HEAD. The c1 recommendation offered two options for restoration, both of which explicitly preserved the inline link: (long) "(the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning notes monitor.py's −29.1 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the published W004 window, conceding the range may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events)." or (short) "(see PRED-095 detail reasoning for the dome author's own potential-cherry-picking admission)." Verified by grep on the L3a consolidated paragraph: "href=\"#pred-pred-095\"" is absent; "−29" / "falls outside the W004 window" are absent. The landed parenthetical ("the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern: monitor.py selection") preserves the substance — the dome-author concession is referenced and attributable — so the rhetorical move ("dome author speaks against the claim") is not lost. But the trailing phrase "monitor.py selection" is cryptic without an inline anchor for a reader to click through, and the concrete cherry-picking evidence (the −29 nT value falling outside the W004 window) is unreachable from the §8b.3 surface. Severity is minor (substance preserved, navigation degraded), not moderate, because the broader §8b.3 already discusses monitor.py formula heterogeneity and the dual-baseline structure two clauses earlier.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) replace the existing trailing "monitor.py selection" with a span carrying the link: "the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern — monitor.py's −29 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the published W004 window"; OR (b) the simpler short form from c1 H4: "(see PRED-095 detail reasoning for the dome author's own potential-cherry-picking admission)". Either adds ~15-25 chars and restores click-through. ~5-minute patch.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c9 hole #1 — §6.14 pointer paragraph still routes the magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument to §4.2.5, but §4.2.5 STILL does not carry that argument. Current §6.14 pointer text: "The magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument — that (baseline × coverage × FSF) is shared arithmetic between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment — lives at §4.2.5." Verified by direct read of §4.2.5 (data/sections.json:part4.html, p4-425 details block, 2542 chars). §4.2.5 contains three structural sub-points — (1) "No dome geometry enters the prediction" (input-chain provenance), (2) "Quiet-condition filtering", (3) "Third attempt". None of these articulate the SHARED-ARITHMETIC non-discrimination argument (that dome W004 and Chapman-Sq treatments produce indistinguishable predictions because the arithmetic is the same). That argument actually lives at §8b.3 (d)(ii) — the (d) Unchanged sub-point (ii) which reads verbatim: "our codified Pass band is a magnitude threshold whose arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus — a Pass in this band does not arbitrate between the two." ISS-2386 (the c1 H2 source hole) was administratively closed at 2026-06-01T01:12:11.611Z with fixed_by="c1-verification-confirmed-correct" and verification_pattern citing SEC-part6-EXP462-P1.c1.json. But that c1 verification only checked the §6.14 pointer paragraph internals (capitalization, FSF vs geometry-weight terminology); it did NOT re-verify that the §4.2.5 destination actually carries the named argument. The destination check fails. A defender following the §6.14 → §4.2.5 link does not find what was promised.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) re-target the §6.14 pointer to §8b.3 (d)(ii) where the argument actually lives: replace "lives at §4.2.5" with "lives at §8b.3 (d)(ii)" — but this creates a forward-ref cycle since §8b.3 also cross-references §6.14; OR (b) extend §4.2.5 with a fourth sub-point making the shared-arithmetic non-discrimination argument explicit (~80 words, e.g., "4. Non-discriminating arithmetic. The eclipse formula ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF applied to the W004 recalibration is arithmetically equivalent to a coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus — both models multiply a baseline by a coverage fraction by a geometric weight. A Pass result therefore does not arbitrate between dome and globe interpretations."); OR (c) reopen ISS-2386 with the c9-strengthened evidence and route it as a new EXP. Option (b) is preferred because it lands the argument in its named canonical home; option (a) creates the cycle problem; option (c) defers without resolution. Severity stays moderate because this is a verifiable navigation error that can be discovered in 30 seconds by a hostile reader.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c9 hole #2 — §8b.3 still cross-references §6.14 for the "internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity" audit, but §6.14's body still does not carry that audit. The current §8b.3 L3a paragraph contains: "the internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity is audited at §6.14 (the κ coupling cluster)". Verified by re-reading §6.14: the new pointer paragraph at the top of §6.14 names the formula heterogeneity but the section body covers only the cross-event κ-vise (Mohe 1997 κ extrapolated to 1999 European + 2009 East Asian SG nulls, WIN-058b storm corollary, "What κ predicts during past eclipses", "What past SG data actually shows"). Token-level grep of §6.14 body: "−18.22" MISSING, "−29 nT" MISSING, "1.672" MISSING (only the κ derivation's 1.677 appears), "inject_ai_layer" MISSING, "latitude_factor" MISSING. The three-formula numerical heterogeneity (BOU −10 nT, W004 −17 to −21 nT, monitor.py −29 nT) lives only partially in WIN-025's detail_evidence. ISS-2384 is OPEN with status=assigned-analyst, so this carry-over is at least tracked. Flagging here to keep visibility on c10 and to signal that the §6.14 pointer paragraph's promise (formula heterogeneity is §6.14's contribution) remains structurally unkept.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to the in-flight ISS-2384 analyst expansion. The analyst should write an EXP adding a 3-5-sentence "Internal formula heterogeneity" h3 to §6.14 (between "How κ was derived" and "What κ predicts during past eclipses") that names monitor.py −29 nT vs published BOU −10 nT vs published W004 −17 to −21 nT, OR redirect the §8b.3 cross-reference to WIN-025 (which already carries the monitor.py −29 nT detail) plus a 1-sentence inline summary in §8b.3. No new ISS needed; ISS-2384 already covers.

stronger_arguments.description: ISS-2388's landed wording ("the critique narrows to the two issues that survive consolidation") is actually STRONGER than the c1 recommendation ("narrowing the critique to formula-selection among coexisting public-facing dome formulas"). The c1 recommendation hard-coded the surviving issues at draft time; the landed version uses a forward-pointer that picks up whatever issues the immediately-following sentence enumerates. As the surviving-issues set evolves (e.g., if a third issue is later flagged), the forward-pointer adapts without rewriting the scope-limit sentence. Lower edit-amplification, same defender-dialogic move. This is a real win, not just a wording variance.
sources: data/sections.json:part8b.html — current L3a paragraph defender-dialogic sentence

stronger_arguments.description: The §6.14 pointer paragraph (PROP-462 P1) is the single load-bearing artifact for routing readers between §8b.3, §6.14, and §4.2.5. Its first claim is wrong (§4.2.5 does not carry the non-discrimination argument it is pointed at). Its second claim is structurally unkept (§6.14's body does not carry the formula-heterogeneity audit). Both moderate holes carry over from c9 because the patch round 2df48ad addressed only the L3a content (ISS-2388 + ISS-2390) and did not touch the §6.14 pointer paragraph or §4.2.5. A coordinated patch that retargets the §6.14 pointer AND extends §4.2.5 (option (b) of c10 H2) AND lands ISS-2384's §6.14 formula-heterogeneity h3 (option (a) of c10 H3) would close all three navigation defects in one operation — cheaper than three sequential patches.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's c1-verification + 2df48ad patch round is the right kind of work — narrow, surgical, queue-driven, and reviewable per-issue. Both ISS-2388 and ISS-2390 patches went into the L3a paragraph with no collateral edits, no novel claim smuggling, no structural breakage. ISS-2388's landed wording is in fact a small improvement over the original c1 recommendation (forward-pointer rather than hard-coded enumeration; see stronger_arguments[0]). ISS-2390's landed wording preserves the rhetorical substance even though the inline link recommended by c1 is absent. The defender-dialogic adjudication character that c9 hole #3 (the moderate finding) was specifically about is now visible on the §8b.3 surface; the PRED-095 cherry-picking concession the c1 H4 finding raised is now textually present. Both patches close their respective ISSs at the substance level. (data/sections.json:part8b.html L3a paragraph verified verbatim; monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json ISS-2388 status='fixed' resolution citing the restored sentence; ISS-2390 status='fixed' resolution citing the restored parenthetical.)
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The closed ISSs do not close the broader navigation problem that c9 H1+H2 flagged. ISS-2386 was administratively closed at 2026-06-01T01:12:11.611Z based on SEC-part6-EXP462-P1.c1.json's pointer-paragraph-internal verification, but that c1 review did NOT verify the destination (§4.2.5). My destination check just now (data/sections.json:part4.html p4-425, 2542 chars) shows §4.2.5 still has only three structural sub-points and zero shared-arithmetic language. So a defender clicking the §6.14 → §4.2.5 link finds nothing. ISS-2384 is at least tracked (open, assigned-analyst) — the §6.14 formula-heterogeneity gap is in the pipeline but not yet closed. ISS-2416 and ISS-2417 (c9 H3 + H4, both minor) remain open. Net: the two queue-item patches landed clean, but the §6.14 pointer paragraph's two promises (route to §4.2.5; §6.14 carries heterogeneity audit) are still unkept. Recommended_action stays minor_edit (the ISS-2390 link restoration is a 5-minute patch; the §6.14 pointer retargeting/§4.2.5 extension is the larger coordinated patch deferred to a new ISS or to ISS-2384's EXP).

deeper_analysis:
Three narrow questions answered for queue_id=438 verification scope: (1) Did the ISS-2388 patch land? — YES. Sentence "We accept the dome-defender scope-limit that monitor.py is internal tooling, not a public commitment; the critique narrows to the two issues that survive consolidation." is in HEAD verbatim, positioned immediately before the "Two issues survive consolidation" forward-pointer. (2) Did the ISS-2390 patch land? — PARTIALLY. Parenthetical "(the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern: monitor.py selection)" is in HEAD verbatim, attached to the "selection from coexisting dome formulas" clause as specified. BUT the inline <a href="#pred-pred-095"> link that the EXP-462 c1 H4 source hole was specifically about is NOT present, AND the concrete "−29 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the published W004 window" content (from the c1 long-form option) is NOT present. The substance is preserved (dome-author cherry-picking concession is referenced and attributable), but click-through navigation to PRED-095 detail is not restored. Severity minor — the rhetorical move survives without the link, the substance is right next to a still-extant WIN-025 cross-reference, and a reader navigating to PRED-095 manually still finds the dome-author's own admission. (3) Did the patch round introduce any collateral breakage? — NO. The L3a paragraph fingerprint grew by ~280 chars relative to c9 (8065 → 8326 for §8b.3 block; 3834 → 4089 for L3a consolidated paragraph), consistent with the two restorations. The four-layer adoption framing (L1-L4), (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) scaffold, (d) Unchanged section, interpretation-A/B Fail-clause double-bind, OTS-wrong-side cross-reference, Timestamp Error tab link — all preserved.

Advocate-mode consideration (dome-defender perspective): A defender reads the restored scope-limit sentence and is mildly placated — we've acknowledged that monitor.py is internal tooling and narrowed the critique. A defender then notices that "monitor.py selection" in the PRED-095 parenthetical is cryptic without context; if they click around for the source they cannot follow an inline link (none present), but a careful reader who navigates to PRED-095 manually does find the dome's own admission (verified: data/predictions.json:PRED-095.detail_reasoning carries "the dome's monitor.py already showed a −29.1 nT value for a prior eclipse that fell outside this window — the range may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events"). So the rhetorical move (dome-author speaks against the claim) is intact. The §6.14 pointer mis-route to §4.2.5 is the strongest defender attack — "your own pointer paragraph cannot keep track of where its argument lives" — and this is the moderate carry-over hole c10 H2 names. The ISS-2384 carry-over (c10 H3) is weaker as a defender attack because the §8b.3 cross-reference is internal-to-our-document and a defender can be told it is in the pipeline (assigned-analyst).

Quantitative verification: char-counts from c9 fingerprint (part8b_html_length 25224, sec_8b3_block_length 8065, consolidated_l3a_length 3834) vs c10 (25505, 8326, 4089). Deltas: +281 / +261 / +255. ISS-2388 sentence is 156 chars verbatim. ISS-2390 parenthetical is 113 chars verbatim. Sum 269 chars — within margin of the observed L3a delta (255). The extra ~10-25 chars at section/file level absorb formatting whitespace and HTML escape entities. Numbers reconcile cleanly. No unaccounted-for prose changes detected.

summary_for_decider:
Queue item 438 verification: ISS-2388 (defender-dialogic scope-limit sentence) and ISS-2390 (PRED-095 cherry-picking parenthetical) BOTH landed in commit 2df48ad as committed in closed-issues resolutions. ISS-2388 is a clean verbatim restoration with a forward-pointer wording that is slightly stronger than the c1 recommendation (lower edit-amplification on future surviving-issue changes). ISS-2390 is a PARTIAL restoration: substance is present (dome-author cherry-picking concession is referenced and attributable), but the inline <a href='#pred-pred-095'> link the c1 H4 hole was specifically about is NOT in HEAD, and the concrete '−29 nT falls outside the W004 window' content from the c1 long-form option is NOT in HEAD. Flagged as minor hole #1 — 5-minute patch to add the link, no new ISS necessary (could be folded into ISS-2390's reopening or handled as a fast-path patch). Two MODERATE carry-over findings from c9: (a) §6.14 pointer still routes magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination to §4.2.5 but §4.2.5 still does not carry the argument — ISS-2386 was administratively closed at 2026-06-01T01:12:11.611Z but the destination check was not performed; recommend either re-targeting the §6.14 pointer to §8b.3 (d)(ii) (creates forward-ref cycle problem) or extending §4.2.5 with a fourth sub-point (preferred; ~80 words). (b) §6.14 still missing formula-heterogeneity audit promised by §8b.3 cross-reference — ISS-2384 is open with status=assigned-analyst, in-flight; flagged for visibility only, no action needed. Two minor carry-overs (ISS-2416, ISS-2417) remain open per c9 H3+H4. Recommended_action: minor_edit (ISS-2390 link restoration is the only fast action; §6.14/§4.2.5 patch larger and may want its own EXP). PROP-009 asserts: queue_id=438 set on this review, matches queue item pushed at 2026-06-01T03:12:07.116Z.

M3 carry-over field (unresolved_prior_cycle): ISS-2386 prior_cycle=9 holes_index=1 (moderate, §6.14 pointer mis-route — needs decider patch or re-open); ISS-2384 prior_cycle=9 holes_index=2 (moderate, §6.14 missing heterogeneity audit — already in-flight, no decider action); ISS-2416 prior_cycle=9 (minor, steel-man kernel — still open); ISS-2417 prior_cycle=9 (minor, INTERMAGNET labels — still open). Decider should at minimum acknowledge ISS-2386 status in the next run: either reopen with c10-strengthened destination evidence or escalate to pending-human if the c1-verification-only closure pattern is acceptable.
